title: Vector-valued gradient over vector inputs throws int64 from non-int-storage cell
goal: Scaling the native EAGLE trainer to 7168 dimensions exposed a real AD compiler/runtime bug: a vector-valued `(gradient fn vec)` path throws `int64 from non-int-storage cell (type=6)`. Scalar `(derivative fn x)` works, and the native qllm_ffi linear backward bridge now sidesteps this for production-scale EAGLE training. Root-fix the vector-gradient path so Eshkol can directly differentiate vector-parameter losses without native helper escapes.
repro: Construct a scalar loss over a mutable/vector parameter and call `(gradient loss weights-vector)`; expected a vector of gradients, actual runtime throws `int64 from non-int-storage cell (type=6)`. Use examples/eagle_train.esk as the scalar/native comparison baseline.
acceptance: minimal vector-valued gradient repro returns a numeric gradient vector instead of throwing
scalar derivative paths from examples/eagle_train.esk still pass
native qllm_ffi bridge tests still pass
campaign_payoff: Removes the compiler-side blocker behind full native EAGLE training without relying on qllm_ffi linear_backward as an escape hatch.
status_note: MERGED #97 / 7c4a43f9549668976637dc33330271833173ca87. Design decision: do not primalize/detach shape dimensions; shape dims/reduction axes are structural integer metadata, so dual values should raise a clean guard-catchable type error. Fix guards reshape direct/list/multi dims and reduction axes, avoids the old 'Attempted to get int64 from non-int-storage cell (type=6)' path, and adds tests/integration/ad_structural_int_guard_test.esk plus CTest smoke. Verification: ICC ready/100 before/after, GPU-off configure/build, JIT focused test PASS, AOT focused test PASS, generated ./a.out PASS, ctest ad_structural_int_guard_runtime_smoke PASS, and CI 14/14 SUCCESS. Existing broader autodiff_tensor_test.esk still fails on current origin/master at line 392 with tensor-sum expected tensor/got ad-node; left out of scope.
